info.title: Postcept API
info.description: Proof-of-Completion for AI agents. Postcept verifies that high-risk agent actions actually completed correctly in the system of record, then issues signed completion receipts.

delete.summary: Delete the organization and erase all its data

post.summary: Create an invitation link
Accept an invitation
Create an API key
Grant an approval

get.summary: Verify the audit log's tamper-evident hash chain

post.summary: Start a subscription
Open the billing portal
Re-validate a stored connector's credentials against its provider
Create a policy
Verify a claimed completion and issue a signed receipt
Re-verify a verification against the live system of record
Reconcile a batch of past verifications (for a scheduled job)

get.summary: Verified Completion Rate over time (daily buckets)
The public key for verifying receipt signatures
What each connector verifies, per action
get.description:
What each connector verifies, per action: the checks that run, the
correlation mechanism, the tests that pin it, and its verified limits. The
status is operational: active, or degraded when the connector returned a
provider state the engine does not recognize (finalized verifications are
withheld until it is patched). Each entry carries safe_completion_reachable:
whether a successful verification through it reaches safe_to_claim_complete,
which requires every check the contract marks required to actually run.

get.summary: The versioned Outcome Contracts verifications are judged by
get.description:
Public registry: each contract's declared postconditions, finality tables,
correlation and duplicate semantics, plus the digest that verifications carry.
Changing any of this is a version bump, never a silent behaviour change.

get.summary: Every signing key, active and retired, for verifying receipts of any age
get.description:
Key rotation never invalidates old receipts: retired public keys stay in
this registry, and a verifier selects the entry matching the receipt's
signing_key_id. Only public halves are ever served.

post.summary: Audit refunds you read yourself, without sending a credential
post.description:
Score refunds the caller already read out of their own system of record.

The relay does the reading, so the provider credential stays in the caller's
environment and Postcept only ever sees the facts. Same rate, same signed badge
as the hosted audit.

post.summary: Run a free, read-only VCR audit on recent refunds

get.summary: The current signed tree head of the public transparency log
A Merkle inclusion proof that a receipt is in the transparency log
An RFC 6962 consistency proof that the log only ever appended
get.description:
Prove the log at `second` (default: current size) is an append-only extension of
the log at `first`, nothing was removed, reordered, or back-dated.

get.summary: Whether the log's checkpoints are witnessed by Sigstore Rekor
get.description:
The latest externally witnessed checkpoint. Postcept signs the tree head;
the anchoring workflow submits it to the public Sigstore Rekor transparency
service and verifies the inclusion material before reporting. A witness
covers everything up to its tree size: the log cannot rewrite history behind
a witnessed checkpoint without the consistency proofs breaking.

get.summary: A signed, portable audit-evidence bundle for the organization

post.summary: Register an operation's observation contracts (digest commitments)

get.summary: Retrieve an operation's preserved observation contracts

post.summary: Validate presented artifacts against the preserved contracts

get.summary: Broken Refund Lab: the deterministic synthetic scenarios

post.summary: Run a synthetic scenario through the real verification engine
post.description:
No account needed. The verification below is produced by the production
`verify()` code path. Only the system of record is synthetic. Runs are not
stored and are excluded from VCR and the transparency log.

post.summary: Register a customer-side relay's public key
post.description:
The relay generates its keypair locally (`postcept-relay keygen`) and only
the public half is registered here. Provider credentials and the signing seed
never leave your environment.

delete.summary: Revoke a relay key (its envelopes stop being accepted immediately)

post.summary: Submit a signed observation envelope from a customer-side relay
post.description:
The envelope must verify against a relay key registered by YOUR org: a
wrong-tenant, unsigned, tampered, expired, or replayed envelope is rejected
with the exact reason. Accepted observations back `connector: "relay"`
verifications, whose receipts name the relay that observed the source.

post.summary: Compose several verifications into one completion view
post.description:
Turn multiple verifications (e.g. a Stripe refund and a Zendesk ticket) into
one composite outcome that represents partial completion and flags premature
closure, a step marked done before a step it depends on is final. The classic
catch: a ticket closed before the refund settled. Reuses existing
verifications. Nothing new is executed or billed.

post.summary: Register an endpoint for signed lifecycle webhooks
post.description:
The signing secret is returned ONCE, here (and on rotate). Store it in your
secret manager. Deliveries are signed `Postcept-Signature: t=...,v1=...`
(HMAC-SHA256 over `"{t}.{raw body}"`), retried with backoff, at-least-once.

get.summary: List webhook endpoints (secrets are never readable)

post.summary: Rotate the signing secret (previous secret signs for 24 more hours)

hmac_key.title: Hmac Key
hmac_key.description: The HMAC key from your Adyen standard-webhook config.

ApiKey.title: ApiKey
ApiKey.description: An organization API key. The secret itself is never returned after creation.

scopes.title: Scopes
scopes.description: Capabilities to grant. Defaults to all scopes.

key.title: Key
key.description: The secret key, shown once. Store it securely now.

ApiKeyScope.title: ApiKeyScope
ApiKeyScope.description: A capability an API key can grant (least privilege).

Approval.title: Approval
Approval.description: A trusted approval for an operation, recorded by a manager.

max_amount_cents.title: Max Amount Cents
max_amount_cents.description: Cap the approval to this amount. None = any amount.

AuditBadge.title: AuditBadge
AuditBadge.description:
A signed, shareable summary of an audit run, tamper-evident, no PII.

The signature covers the canonical body (type, label, account_ref, connector,
sampled, verified_completion_rate, issued_at) and is verifiable with the public
signing key, so a published badge can't be forged or inflated.

AuditEvent.title: AuditEvent
AuditEvent.description: An append-only record of something that happened in an organization.

AuditFinding.title: AuditFinding
AuditFinding.description: One sampled action and how the audit classified it. PII-free by construction.

AuditReport.title: AuditReport
AuditReport.description: The result of a read-only VCR audit. Stateless, nothing here is persisted.

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: A read-only Stripe (restricted) API key. Used once, never stored.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: How many recent refunds to sample (max 100).

agent_label.title: Agent Label
agent_label.description: Optional label shown on the shareable badge.

AuditRequest.title: AuditRequest
AuditRequest.description:
A free, read-only VCR audit of an organization's recent refunds.

The credential is used once to sample the system of record and is never stored.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Whether billing is configured on the server.

BillingState.title: BillingState
BillingState.description: An organization's billing status and usage this period.

subscription_id.title: Subscription Id
subscription_id.description: The subscription the agent claims it cancelled.

customer.title: Customer
customer.description: Customer id or email the subscription belongs to.

CancellationClaim.title: CancellationClaim
CancellationClaim.description: An agent's claim that it cancelled a subscription.

status.title: Status
status.description: ok = the credential still authenticates, invalid = the provider rejected it (revoked or rotated), unavailable = the provider couldn't be reached (transient).

ConnectorHealth.title: ConnectorHealth
ConnectorHealth.description: The result of re-validating a stored connector against its provider on demand.

account_ref.title: Account Ref
account_ref.description: Stable, non-secret fingerprint of the account.

ConnectorInfo.title: ConnectorInfo
ConnectorInfo.description: A configured organization connector, the secret credentials are never returned.

ConnectorType.title: ConnectorType
ConnectorType.description: A configurable per-org system-of-record connector (excludes the dev mock).

first_size.title: First Size
first_size.description: Size of the earlier tree head being checked.

second_size.title: Second Size
second_size.description: Size of the later (current) tree head.

ConsistencyProof.title: ConsistencyProof
ConsistencyProof.description:
Proof that the log at `second_size` is an append-only extension of the log at
`first_size`, same leaves 0..first_size, nothing removed or rewritten (RFC 6962
§2.1.2). A holder of an earlier signed tree head uses it to confirm the operator
never forked or back-dated the log.

ContractRef.title: ContractRef
ContractRef.description:
Which versioned Outcome Contract a verification was judged by. The digest
covers the contract's canonical JSON (see GET /v1/contracts). It becomes part
of the receipt's signed body in receipt v3.

CorrelationMethod.title: CorrelationMethod
CorrelationMethod.description: How the claimed action was located in the system of record.

CorrelationStrength.title: CorrelationStrength
CorrelationStrength.description:
How strong the link is between the claim and the observed record. High-risk
decisions should require `deterministic`. A heuristic link must never be
presented as certainty.

CurrentOrg.title: CurrentOrg
CurrentOrg.description: The caller's organization and their role in it.

EvidenceExport.title: EvidenceExport
EvidenceExport.description:
A portable, signed audit-evidence bundle for an organization. Helps create
audit evidence: every part is independently verifiable, the signed receipts,
the tamper-evident (hash-chained) audit log, and the transparency-log inclusion
proofs, and the signed manifest binds them together.

audit_chain_intact.title: Audit Chain Intact
audit_chain_intact.description: Whether the org's audit hash chain verified.

transparency_root.title: Transparency Root
transparency_root.description: Hex Merkle root of the transparency log at export.

content_digest.title: Content Digest
content_digest.description: SHA-256 hex over the bundle's receipts (sorted id+signature).

EvidenceManifest.title: EvidenceManifest
EvidenceManifest.description:
A signed summary binding an evidence export to its contents, so the bundle
can't be added to, trimmed, or swapped without breaking the signature.

subdomain.title: Subdomain
subdomain.description: The subdomain, e.g. acme for acme.gorgias.com.

leaf_index.title: Leaf Index
leaf_index.description: 0-based position of the receipt in the log.

leaf_hash.title: Leaf Hash
leaf_hash.description: Hex RFC 6962 leaf hash = SHA256(0x00 || id || 0x0a || sig).

InclusionProof.title: InclusionProof
InclusionProof.description:
Proof that a receipt is included in the transparency log at a given tree size:
a Merkle audit path from the receipt's leaf up to the signed root.

Invite.title: Invite
Invite.description: A pending invitation to join an organization (shared as a link).

account.title: Account
account.description: The account id in URL host form, e.g. 1234567 or 1234567-sb1.

digest.title: Digest
digest.description: SHA-256 hex of the artifact value (one-way, not the value).

ObservationContract.title: ObservationContract
ObservationContract.description: A registered contract, a digest commitment, no secret material.

value.title: Value
value.description: The artifact value. Stored only as a digest.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: For session_token: when it expires (tracked, validated).

signature.title: Signature
signature.description: For webhook_payload: the HMAC to verify later, hex.

ObservationContractInput.title: ObservationContractInput
ObservationContractInput.description: An artifact to register. Only its digest is stored, never the value itself.

ObservationContractType.title: ObservationContractType
ObservationContractType.description:
A fragile operation artifact whose integrity Postcept preserves across an
operation's retries, branches, and handoffs.

secret.title: Secret
secret.description: For webhook_payload: the HMAC secret. Used once, never stored.

account_ref.title: Account Ref
account_ref.description: A stable, non-secret fingerprint of the audited account.

agent_label.title: Agent Label
agent_label.description: Optional label shown on the shareable badge.

ObservedAuditRequest.title: ObservedAuditRequest
ObservedAuditRequest.description:
A VCR audit over refunds the caller has already read themselves.

The credential never reaches Postcept: the relay reads the system of record
inside your environment and sends only these facts. Postcept scores them and
signs the badge.

operation_ref.title: Operation Ref
operation_ref.description: The operation this refund belongs to, used to detect a double refund.

ObservedRefund.title: ObservedRefund
ObservedRefund.description:
A refund as it was read out of the system of record, by something that already
holds the credential. Carries no customer identity and no credential.

OperationContext.title: OperationContext
OperationContext.description: The preserved observation contracts for one operation.

intact.title: Intact
intact.description: True iff every presented contract is preserved.

client_id.title: Client Id
client_id.description: A PayPal REST app client id.

PolicyType.title: PolicyType
PolicyType.description: A configurable policy rule applied during verification.

PostconditionCategory.title: PostconditionCategory
PostconditionCategory.description: Drives classification: which kind of completion failure a check represents.

Receipt.title: Receipt
Receipt.description: A signed, tamper-evident proof of what was checked and how it classified.

ReconcileSummary.title: ReconcileSummary
ReconcileSummary.description: Outcome counts from a reconciliation batch run.

refund_id.title: Refund Id
refund_id.description: The refund id the agent claims it created, e.g. re_4md82k.

customer.title: Customer
customer.description: Customer id or email the refund should belong to.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Free-form context for policy checks (approval id, reason, ...).

RefundClaim.title: RefundClaim
RefundClaim.description: An agent's claim that it refunded a charge.

public_key.title: Public Key
public_key.description: Base64 raw Ed25519 PUBLIC key. The seed stays with you.

ReviewStatus.title: ReviewStatus
ReviewStatus.description: Recovery workflow state for a verification.

status.description: One of: open, in_review, resolved, dismissed.

Role.title: Role
Role.description: A member's role within an organization.

shop.title: Shop
shop.description: The shop subdomain, e.g. acme for acme.myshopify.com.

tree_size.title: Tree Size
tree_size.description: Number of receipts in the log this head covers.

signature.title: Signature
signature.description: Ed25519 signature over {type, tree_size, root_hash, timestamp}.

SignedTreeHead.title: SignedTreeHead
SignedTreeHead.description:
A signed commitment to the whole transparency log at a point in time: the
Merkle root over all logged receipts, the tree size, and a timestamp. Monotonic
and signed, so the log can't be silently rewritten or back-dated.

SigningKey.title: SigningKey
SigningKey.description: The public half of the receipt signing key, clients verify receipts with this.

SigningKeyEntry.title: SigningKeyEntry
SigningKeyEntry.description: A registry entry: the active key or a retired one kept for verification.

SigningKeyRegistry.title: SigningKeyRegistry
SigningKeyRegistry.description:
Every public key a receipt may be signed with, so rotating the signing key
never breaks verification of receipts the old key signed. Select the entry
whose key_id matches the receipt's signing_key_id.

ticket_id.title: Ticket Id
ticket_id.description: The support ticket the agent claims it resolved.

status.title: Status
status.description: The expected ticket status, e.g. solved.

customer.title: Customer
customer.description: Requester email, if it should match.

TicketClaim.title: TicketClaim
TicketClaim.description: An agent's claim that it resolved a support ticket.

VcrSummary.title: VcrSummary
VcrSummary.description: Verified Completion Rate, verified completions over all claimed completions.

next_reverify_at.title: Next Reverify At
next_reverify_at.description: The contract's evidence-freshness deadline: when this verification is next re-verified against the system of record.

decision.title: Decision
decision.description: One machine-actionable word for what happened, derived from the result and the lifecycle: verified, pending, not_observed, failed, mismatched, duplicated, reversed, policy_failed, indeterminate, or unavailable.

observation_assurance.title: Observation Assurance
observation_assurance.description: How the underlying state was observed: managed (Postcept read the provider with scoped credentials), relay_attested (your relay read and signed the observation), or webhook_attested (a provider-signed webhook was verified).

VerificationLifecycle.title: VerificationLifecycle
VerificationLifecycle.description:
Where the observed action sits in the provider's lifecycle, separate from the
outcome verdict. "Refund is still pending" and "refund never happened" both
classify as an *incomplete* outcome, but they are different situations. The
lifecycle dimension tells them apart.

operation_id.title: Operation Id
operation_id.description: Stable id for the agent operation, across retries.

test.title: Test
test.description: Sandbox mode: verify against the deterministic mock connector instead of a live system of record. Test verifications are excluded from your Verified Completion Rate.

observations.title: Observations
observations.description: Optional: operation artifacts to validate against this operation's preserved observation contracts. A failure adds a context_intact postcondition to the receipt.

VerificationResult.title: VerificationResult
VerificationResult.description: How a claimed completion was classified against the system of record.

WebhookEndpointCreate.title: WebhookEndpointCreate
WebhookEndpointCreate.description: Register an endpoint to receive signed lifecycle webhooks.

secret.title: Secret
secret.description: HMAC signing secret (pcpt_whsec_...). Store it now.

WebhookEndpointWithSecret.title: WebhookEndpointWithSecret
WebhookEndpointWithSecret.description: Returned ONLY on create or rotate. The secret is never readable afterwards.

current.title: Current
current.description: Whether the witnessed checkpoint covers the log's current tree size.

WitnessStatus.title: WitnessStatus
WitnessStatus.description: The public witness state of the transparency log.

id.title: Id
id.description: A name for this step within the workflow, e.g. 'refund'.

verification_id.title: Verification Id
verification_id.description: An existing verification to include as this step.

depends_on.title: Depends On
depends_on.description: Step ids this step depends on. It isn't safely complete until they are.

subdomain.title: Subdomain
subdomain.description: The subdomain, e.g. acme for acme.zendesk.com.

HTTPBearer.description: Supabase user token or pcpt_sk_ API key

tags.name: verifications
tags.description: Verify agent actions against the system of record and read receipts.

tags.name: vcr-audit
tags.description: Verified Completion Rate and read-only refund audits.

tags.name: connectors
tags.description: Configure read-only system-of-record connections (validated on connect).

tags.name: transparency
tags.description: Public transparency log: tree head and per-receipt inclusion proofs.

tags.name: api-keys
tags.description: Create and revoke scoped API keys.

tags.name: policies
tags.description: Policy postconditions an action must satisfy.

tags.name: observations
tags.description: Operation observation contracts, preserved across retries and handoffs.

tags.name: webhooks
tags.description: Inbound provider webhooks (e.g. Adyen refund events).

tags.name: contact
tags.description: Marketing-site contact enquiries and newsletter sign-ups.
